authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1926.20 - General safety and health provisions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA is the primary U.S. federal construction-safety regulator. This standard defines safety programs, frequent inspections, and competent-person oversight that can block handoff and field progression.

authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1926.752 - Site layout, site-specific erection plan and construction sequence
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA defines written notifications and sequencing responsibilities between controlling contractors and erectors before work begins, making it authoritative for trade-to-trade readiness and coordination boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Trade sequencing and subcontractor coordination gating

authoritative_sources.title: Residential Final Inspection Quick Sheet
authoritative_sources.publisher: Seattle Department of Construction and Inspections
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: An official building-department final-inspection guide that names permit-card, approved-plan, access, address, and final-readiness conditions before permit completion.

authoritative_sources.title: After You Get a Permit
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NYC DOB is an official authority having jurisdiction and its permit-holder guidance describes permit posting, approved-plan availability, required inspections, signoff, and permit closeout steps.
used_for: permit sign-off routing and closure boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: GSA Form 184A/184C Construction Progress Report and Instructions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA's official federal progress-reporting form and instructions define how pending issues, deficiencies, schedule status, and coordination notes are captured in construction administration.

authoritative_sources.title: PBS-PQ100 Project Planning Guide
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA's official project planning and closeout guide defines turnover, training, O&M manuals, as-builts, deficiency correction, and final completion expectations that gate closeout handoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Closeout package and turnover completion gating

authoritative_sources.title: Resident Management System Contractor Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USACE's official contractor guide governs schedule updates, daily reports, submittals, RFIs, and closeout data in RMS/QCS, making it authoritative for federal construction handoff and evidence practices.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Work-order administration and cross-team handoff packaging
used_for: work-order and closeout data system boundaries

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Normalize request scope, work order, trade, permit, inspection, site-access, and subcontract owner context before any routing or closure output is produced.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Site-readiness, safety, and permit screening
workflow_stages.description: Check whether site access, approved plans, permit status, pre-task safety documents, and field-readiness controls are present before work or mobilization is handed off.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team coordination and ownership resolution
workflow_stages.description: Identify prior owner, next owner, duplicated ownership, schedule conflicts, and required artifact completeness across GC, field-service, superintendent, permitting, inspection, and subcontractor teams.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Inspection, deficiency, and closeout blocker handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve correction notices, punch-list items, turnover documents, and final-inspection gates as blockers until the correct owner and evidence set are explicit.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Authority-boundary escalation and closure confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate whenever the request drifts into licensed trade judgment, design interpretation, code interpretation, permit approval, or safety-critical signoff, and confirm closure only after destination-owner acceptance is explicit.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

artifacts: work orders, scopes of work, dispatch tickets, subcontractor assignments, and service requests
issued permits, permit cards, approved plans, inspection cards, correction notices, and AHJ correspondence
three-week look-ahead schedules, baseline schedules, milestone logs, owner maps, and dependency trackers
site logs, pre-task plans, JHAs or JSAs, safety observations, incident notes, and stop-work notices
RFIs, submittals, design clarifications, change orders, punch lists, deficiency logs, and turnover checklists
as-built drawings, O&M manuals, test and balancing records, commissioning records, and closeout packages
systems_of_record: scheduling system or look-ahead planning board

allowed: Classify subcontractor coordination requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing permit, site, inspection, schedule, or closeout prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, systems-of-record links, and authoritative records required to continue safely.
Track duplicated ownership, stale handoffs, and dependency failures across permitting, inspection, field, safety, and subcontractor teams.
Produce handoff summaries, blocker logs, routing confirmations, and closure confirmations when evidence is complete.
escalate: Permit status, inspection requirements, safety conditions, or closeout evidence are missing or materially conflict across sources of record.
The request requires permit approval, code interpretation, licensed trade judgment, engineering review, safety signoff, or change-order approval.
Public guidance, tenant policy, contract terms, or jurisdictional instructions materially conflict on the next step.
A stop-work condition, correction notice, inspection failure, or closeout deficiency changes whether the work may proceed.
refuse: Authorize work to proceed without required permit, site-readiness, safety, or inspection prerequisites.
Approve permit signoff, code compliance, licensed trade signoff, engineer-of-record decisions, or design interpretation.
Close a handoff or closeout package without destination-owner acceptance and required artifact completeness.

domain_failure_modes: subcontractor work is handed off without permit-card, approved-plan, or site-access evidence and the crew arrives unable to start safely
inspection preparation ownership is ambiguous across superintendent, project engineer, and subcontractor teams so the inspection window is missed
the lane marks work complete even though correction notices, punch-list items, or turnover documents remain unresolved
schedule pressure causes the lane to imply permit approval, code interpretation, or licensed trade signoff that it does not own
a changed site-safety condition or stop-work notice is treated as routine scheduling noise and the wrong team proceeds
tenant-specific PMIS or field-service routing is missing, so the lane guesses the next owner and drops evidence continuity

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Request that implies licensed trade or code-interpretation authority
Work is handed off without required context or artifacts
Multiple teams appear to own the same step
The lane is asked to absorb execution work instead of coordinating it
Ownership is ambiguous across multiple teams
A handoff is attempted without the required context

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Local AHJ permit, inspection, and certificate requirements vary materially by jurisdiction, project type, and tenant delivery model.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack uses OSHA, Seattle, NYC, GSA, and USACE sources as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve jurisdiction, permit type, tenant signoff rules, and local inspection sequence before applying tenant-specific outcomes.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Licensed trade boundaries and self-perform versus subcontract delivery rules may differ by tenant contract model and union or licensing environment.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline constrains the lane from assuming licensed trade authority. Runtime must retrieve tenant owner maps and trade-boundary rules before routing execution-adjacent requests.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling instead of only generic coordination language.
The lane stops at coordination. Permit approval, code interpretation, licensed trade signoff, design judgment, and safety-critical release remain outside scope.
